name: hypogenic
tagline: Wraps the ChicagoHAI 'hypogenic' pip package to auto-generate and test scientific hypotheses from data and literature via LLM APIs.
verdict: Clean, well-documented research-automation skill: no hidden instructions, secret theft, or destructive commands found. It does pip-install an external package, clone two GitHub dataset repos, and run third-party GROBID setup scripts from those clones, so trust partly rests on those external sources. By design it sends your dataset text to OpenAI/Anthropic AP

highlights.summary: Instructs running third-party GROBID setup/run scripts (lines 235, 244) from a separately cloned repo (github.com/ChicagoHAI/hypothesis-generation) without any integrity check (hash/pin) before execut

Installs an external pip package (`uv pip install hypogenic`, line 20) and clones two additional GitHub repos for datasets (lines 23, 134, 137, 615, 618) plus runs GROBID via a third cloned repo — thr

At 654 lines the file is well beyond a lean context budget, with substantial space spent on marketing-style 'Proven Results'/'Expected Outcomes' stats (lines 78-82, 420-426) and three full BibTeX cita
